hypotheses.title: Melatonin-Induced Diatoxanthin Accumulation as a Thermal NPQ Buffer in Symbiodiniaceae
hypotheses.mechanism: Melatonin enhances NPQ in Symbiodiniaceae by promoting diadinoxanthin-to-diatoxanthin deepoxidation via DDE, analogous to melatonin-enhanced violaxanthin deepoxidation in plants. Proposed route: melatonin at signaling concentrations activates a MAPK-dependent transcriptional program that upregulates DDE expression and/or enhances cyclic electron flow for lumen acidification. Roopin 2013 photoprotection enhancement is consistent with this mechanism.
hypotheses.prediction: Heat-stressed (32C) Symbiodiniaceae pretreated with 10 uM melatonin show higher Dt/(Dd+Dt) ratio, higher NPQ values, and slower Fv/Fm decline vs controls. Durusdinium shows higher baseline melatonin and constitutive DPS than Cladocopium.
hypotheses.test_protocol: Culture C. goreaui and D. trenchii at 26C. Melatonin pretreatment (0, 1, 10, 100 uM, 12h nocturnal). Ramp to 32C. Measure Fv/Fm, NPQ (PAM), pigments (HPLC: Dd, Dt), ROS (DCFDA), endogenous melatonin (LC-MS/MS) at 0-72h. 4-6 weeks effort.
hypotheses.counter_evidence: Melatonin may enhance photoprotection via mechanisms other than xanthophyll deepoxidation. DDE may not be transcriptionally regulated. MAPK signaling in Symbiodiniaceae may not respond to melatonin.

hypotheses.title: Melatonin-AFMK-AMK Cascade Amplification Compensates for Low GSH in Heat-Stressed Symbiodiniaceae Chloroplasts
hypotheses.mechanism: The melatonin -> AFMK -> AMK scavenging cascade generates ~4-10 ROS scavenged per melatonin molecule via non-enzymatic sequential oxidation. Under heat stress, Symbiodiniaceae experience a GSH crash as ROS production exceeds GR regeneration capacity. At stress-elevated concentrations (~215 uM per Antolin 1997), the cascade provides ~2 mM-equivalent scavenging, comparable to the GSH pool.
hypotheses.prediction: During thermal ramp (26-32C, 48h), declining GSH/GSSG simultaneous with rising melatonin/AFMK/AMK. Crossover corresponds to Fv/Fm inflection. Exogenous melatonin delays inflection by ~12h.
hypotheses.test_protocol: Culture C. goreaui. Thermal ramp to 32C. Time-series (0-48h): GSH/GSSG, melatonin/AFMK/AMK (LC-MS/MS), Fv/Fm. Parallel +10 uM melatonin, +5 mM GSH, vehicle. 6-8 weeks.
hypotheses.counter_evidence: 215 uM from Gonyaulax cold stress may not transfer to Symbiodiniaceae heat stress. Cascade rate may be too slow vs GR recycling. Direct scavenging negligible (0.04% OH flux).

hypotheses.title: Melatonin Entrains a Nocturnal Antioxidant Pre-Loading Program in Symbiodiniaceae That Determines Next-Day Thermal Resilience
hypotheses.mechanism: Roopin 2013 shows nocturnal melatonin peaks in Symbiodinium. In plants, melatonin upregulates SOD, CAT, APX, GR via MAPK. Nocturnal melatonin pre-loads antioxidant enzymes before dawn PSII ROS production. Under heat stress, nocturnal heat disrupts melatonin production and pre-loading, making nighttime temperature a stronger bleaching predictor than daytime temperature.
hypotheses.prediction: (a) Antioxidant gene transcripts peak pre-dawn. (b) Hot nights (32C night/26C day) worse than hot days (26C night/32C day). (c) Melatonin during scotophase rescues hot-night effect.
hypotheses.test_protocol: C. goreaui 12:12 L:D. 48h time-series qPCR + LC-MS/MS every 4h. Asymmetric heat groups. Fv/Fm dawn/midday x 5 days. Rescue +10 uM melatonin pre-lights-off. 8-12 weeks.
hypotheses.counter_evidence: Dinoflagellate gene regulation predominantly post-transcriptional. Roopin 2013 peak is photocycle-driven not circadian. Night heat may worsen bleaching via D1 repair unrelated to melatonin.

hypotheses.title: Melatonin as a Diffusible Symbiont-to-Host Redox Signal That Primes Coral Innate Immunity Against Bleaching
hypotheses.mechanism: Melatonin is amphiphilic (logP ~1.0), crossing membranes freely. By analogy to plant chloroplast-to-nucleus retrograde signaling, Symbiodiniaceae melatonin diffuses into coral host cytoplasm, modulating NF-kB innate immune pathways. Normal melatonin flux = immune tolerance. Altered flux under heat = immune activation = symbiont expulsion = bleaching.
hypotheses.prediction: (a) Melatonin detectable in host tissue tracking symbiont levels. (b) Exogenous melatonin suppresses NF-kB in aposymbiotic corals. (c) Melatonin decline precedes symbiont expulsion.
hypotheses.test_protocol: Exaiptasia pallida. LC-MS/MS melatonin in symbiont/host fractions. Exogenous melatonin to aposymbiotic: qPCR immune genes. Heat stress time-series. 12-16 weeks.
hypotheses.counter_evidence: Melatonin may not exit symbiosome meaningfully. NF-kB activation may be consequence not cause. Melatonin-NF-kB effects context-dependent. >600 Mya divergence.

hypotheses.title: TPH/AANAT Homolog Expression in Symbiodiniaceae Transcriptomes Predicts Genus-Level Thermal Tolerance
hypotheses.mechanism: Camp et al. 2022 (PRJNA723630) has RNAseq for 3 Symbiodiniaceae genera at 26C vs 32C but tryptophan pathway genes were NOT analyzed. Melatonin exists in Symbiodinium (Roopin 2013), TPH is expressed in dinoflagellates (Balzer & Hardeland 1996). Published unmeasured variable: thermally tolerant Durusdinium should have higher TPH/AANAT than sensitive Cladocopium.
hypotheses.prediction: (a) TPH/AANAT identifiable by BLAST/HMMER. (b) Durusdinium >2-fold higher expression at 26C. (c) Upregulated at 32C in Durusdinium only.
hypotheses.test_protocol: Download PRJNA723630. BLAST/HMMER for TPH/AANAT. Quantify TPM. DESeq2 differential expression. qPCR validation. 2-4 weeks computational + 4 weeks qPCR.
hypotheses.counter_evidence: Dinoflagellate genomes massive, poorly annotated. Limited transcriptional regulation (<2-fold). Thermal tolerance may be unrelated to melatonin. AANAT confounded by general NAT superfamily.

hypotheses.title: Heat-Stress-Induced IDO-Mediated Tryptophan Diversion Depletes Symbiodiniaceae Melatonin Precursor Pool, Accelerating Bleaching
hypotheses.mechanism: Tryptophan branchpoint: TPH route (melatonin) vs IDO route (kynurenine). STRING IDO1-AANAT 0.931 reflects metabolic competition. If thermal stress upregulates IDO in Symbiodiniaceae, tryptophan diverted from melatonin to kynurenine. Positive feedback: heat -> IDO up -> melatonin down -> less defense -> more ROS -> more IDO.
hypotheses.prediction: (a) Heat-stressed Symbiodiniaceae: increased kynurenine, decreased melatonin. (b) Tryptophan supplementation rescues melatonin and Fv/Fm. (c) IDO inhibitor maintains melatonin.
hypotheses.test_protocol: C. goreaui at 26C/32C. LC-MS/MS tryptophan metabolite panel (0-48h). Rescue: +500 uM Trp, +100 uM 1-methyltryptophan. Fv/Fm + ROS. 8-10 weeks.
hypotheses.counter_evidence: Dinoflagellates may lack stress-inducible IDO. Km comparison from human enzymes. Tryptophan may not be limiting. 1-methyltryptophan may not work on dinoflagellate IDO.

hypotheses.title: D1 Protein Repair Cycle Acceleration by Melatonin Extends Symbiodiniaceae PSII Functional Lifetime Under Thermal Stress
hypotheses.mechanism: Bleaching is a D1 repair-rate problem: damage exceeds repair under heat (increased 1O2, heat-labile EF-Tu). In plants melatonin maintains PSII efficiency partly via D1 turnover enhancement. Melatonin may enhance psbA translation or FtsH activity in Symbiodiniaceae. 20-30% repair rate increase could shift bleaching threshold 1-2C.
hypotheses.prediction: (a) Faster Fv/Fm recovery after lincomycin washout with melatonin. (b) Higher D1 protein levels. (c) Bleaching threshold +1-2C.
hypotheses.test_protocol: C. goreaui. Lincomycin block (500 ug/mL) at 26C/32C +/- melatonin. Fv/Fm decline (damage) and recovery (repair). Western blot D1. Optional psbA qPCR. 6-8 weeks.
hypotheses.counter_evidence: Plant melatonin Fv/Fm effect may be ROS scavenging not D1 repair. Dinoflagellate plastid minicircles differ from plants. EF-Tu sensitivity not addressable by melatonin.

hypotheses.title: Genus-Specific Melatonin Secretion Rates Determine Symbiodiniaceae Competitive Outcomes Within the Same Coral Colony During Thermal Stress
hypotheses.mechanism: Corals host multiple Symbiodiniaceae genera. Thermally tolerant Durusdinium replaces Cladocopium during bleaching (symbiont shuffling). Melatonin as competitive mechanism: higher-producing Durusdinium creates local antioxidant microenvironment and signals health to host. Lower-producing Cladocopium preferentially expelled. Competitive outcome by relative melatonin output.
hypotheses.prediction: (a) Higher-melatonin genus increases during heat stress. (b) Exogenous melatonin prevents shuffling. (c) Higher-producing genus conditions medium to reduce ROS.
hypotheses.test_protocol: Exaiptasia with mixed symbionts. Heat 32C x 14d +/- melatonin. qPCR genus ratios. Melatonin in medium. Transwell co-culture. 16-20 weeks.
hypotheses.counter_evidence: Shuffling may be purely differential death. Extracellular melatonin dilution below threshold. Host cannot detect cell-level gradients. Melatonin production variable within genera.
